title: senecio aureus var. balsamitae
intro: Senecio aureus var. balsamitae, also known as Golden Ragwort, is a perennial plant belonging to the daisy family, characterized by its golden-yellow flowers and serrated leaves. Traditionally, Golden Ragwort has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant is also believed to have health benefits, such as reducing fever and promoting digestion, due to its bitter compounds and alkaloids. Golden Ragwort can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for its herbal remedies, although its use should be cautious due to potential toxicity and interactions with certain medications.
